title: Enhancing Wide-Bandgap Triple-Halide Perovskites for Tandem Solar Cells by 0.5% Formate and Zn(II) Doping

claims.text: The A-site cation subscripts of the stated composition (Cs0.21 FA0.74 MA0.05) sum to 1.00, as required for a normalized ABX3 perovskite.
claims.predicate: Add the three A-site subscripts and check they equal 1.00.

verification.verifier_needed: Arithmetic sum of stoichiometric subscripts (stoichiometry/total check).
verification.rationale: 0.21+0.74+0.05 = 1.00 exactly. Off-the-shelf table-total / stoichiometry check covers this.

claims.text: The X-site halide subscripts of the stated composition (I0.81 Br0.14 Cl0.05) sum to 1.00.
claims.predicate: Add the three halide subscripts and check they equal 1.00.

verification.verifier_needed: Arithmetic sum of stoichiometric subscripts (stoichiometry/total check).
verification.rationale: 0.81+0.14+0.05 = 1.00 exactly. Off-the-shelf stoichiometry/total check covers this.

claims.text: Mariotti's composition (Cs0.22 FA0.78)Pb(I0.85 Br0.15)3 + 5 mol% MAPbCl3 re-writes (assuming full incorporation, no decomposition) to (Cs0.21 FA0.74 MA0.05)Pb(I0.81 Br0.14 Cl0.05)3.
claims.quote: We can re-write the composition used by Mariotti et al., (Cs0.22 FA0.78 )Pb(I0.85 Br0.15 )3 + 5% mol MAPbCl3 , into a more common description: we get (Cs0.21 FA0.74 MA0.05 )Pb(I0.81 Br0.14 Cl0.05 )3 , thereby assuming that all MAPbCl3 is incorporated into the perovskite matrix
claims.predicate: Recompute: combine 1 mol base perovskite + 0.05 mol MAPbCl3; total Pb = 1.05; normalize each A-site and X-site amount per Pb; compare to stated formula.

verification.verifier_needed: Mass-balance / stoichiometry recomputation of mixed perovskite composition.
verification.rationale: Recompute confirms: Cs 0.2095->0.21, FA 0.7429->0.74, MA 0.0476->0.05, I 0.8095->0.81, Br 0.1429->0.14, Cl 0.0476->0.05. Every value rounds to the stated subscript. Only caveat: MA and Cl each round 0.0476->0.05 (mild upward rounding); fully consistent given 2-decimal precision. Off-the-shelf mass-balance/stoichiometry verifier settles it.

claims.text: Total doping of 1% comprises Zn(II) at 0.5 mol% and formate (Fo-) at 0.5 mol% (relative to Pb), i.e., 0.5% + 0.5% = 1%.
claims.quote: the optimal total doping concentration of zinc ions (Zn(II)) and formate anions (Fo-) is determined to be 1% relative to Pb(II)
claims.predicate: Add the two dopant percentages and check they equal the stated 1% total.

evidence.quote: doping with Zn(II) (0.5 mol%), as well as for Fo- (0.5 mol% vs. Pb(II))

verification.verifier_needed: Arithmetic sum (percent components add to stated total).
verification.rationale: 0.5% + 0.5% = 1% total. Internally consistent across abstract, results, and conclusions. Off-the-shelf arithmetic/total check covers this.

claims.text: A maximum absolute PLQY of 14% was obtained for the wide-bandgap perovskite on quartz at 1% total doping.
claims.quote: By using calibrated absolute photoluminescence quantum yield equipment, a maximum PLQY of 14% was obtained for wide-bandgap perovskite on quartz
claims.predicate: Confirm the prose number 14% matches the value reported in the Figure 5 caption and is internally consistent with the abstract.

evidence.quote: Absolute photoluminescence quantum yield measurement data (PLQY = 14%)
Photoluminescence quantum yields (up to 14% on quartz)

verification.verifier_needed: Prose-vs-figure-caption consistency check (the number 14% appears identically in abstract, body, Figure 5 title and caption).
verification.rationale: 14% is stated consistently in abstract, body text, Figure 5 title block, and caption. The underlying value is an integrated-sphere PLQY; the raw spectrometer integral cannot be recomputed from the rendered figure, so consistency (not first-principles recompute) is what is verifiable. Prose-vs-figure check supports the claim.

claims.text: The 14% PLQY reported here is ~3.7x higher than the 3.75% PLQY Mariotti et al. reported for their films on quartz.
claims.quote: It has to be noted that Mariotti et al. [6] reported 3.75% as PLQY for their films on quartz (and a PL lifetime of ~2 us, by tail-fit).
claims.predicate: Compute the ratio 14/3.75 and verify it is consistent with the qualitative 'higher efficiency' claim; cross-check the 3.75% value against ref [6].

evidence.quote: Mariotti et al. [6] reported 3.75% as PLQY for their films on quartz

verification.verifier_needed: Percent-change / ratio arithmetic (internal); literature cross-check of the 3.75% value against Mariotti Science 2023 (ref [6]) for the external part.
verification.rationale: Internal ratio 14/3.75 = 3.73x is arithmetically sound and supports the qualitative improvement statement (T0/T2 part supported). The external value 3.75% attributed to ref [6] is a T5 literature lookup not settled here; comparison is also cross-substrate-consistent (both on quartz).

claims.text: The best optimally doped film shows a PL lifetime of 6.84 +/- 1.1 us (stretched-exponential, beta = 0.84), longer than reference materials at 3.65 +/- 0.8 us (2024) and 1.57 +/- 0.1 us (2019).
claims.quote: the red trace shows our 2025 material with a lifetime of 6.84 +/- 1.1 us, compared to 3.65 +/- 0.8 us (2024, blue) and 1.57 +/- 0.1 us (2019, green)
claims.predicate: Confirm the three lifetimes and uncertainties in the body text exactly match the tau(1)/tau(2)/tau(3) values printed in Figure 6 and its caption, and that ordering (6.84 > 3.65 > 1.57) supports the 'progressively longer' claim.

evidence.quote: Beta values are, respectively, 0.90 (1.57 us) [9], 0.77 (3.65 us) [11], and 0.84 (6.84 us)

verification.verifier_needed: Prose-vs-figure (and caption) numeric consistency check across body, Figure 6 inset, and caption.
verification.rationale: Body text 6.84/3.65/1.57 us exactly match Figure 6 inset tau values and caption; ordering supports the 'progressively longer lifetime' statement. The underlying stretched-exponential fit cannot be re-fit from the figure, but the cross-location consistency is verifiable and clean.

claims.text: Both PL intensity (relative PLQY) and PL lifetime histogram peak position are maximal at 1% total doping in the composition series (Figure 3).
claims.quote: Both metrics show their highest PL performance at a total doping level of 1%.
claims.predicate: Inspect Figure 3: verify the red (relative PLQY) and blue (PL-lifetime histogram max x2) curves both attain their maximum at the x = 1.0% data point.

evidence.quote: Relative photoluminescence quantum yields (in red, left y-axis) and the peak positions of PL lifetime-histogram (in blue, right y-axis) ... as a function of total doping concentration

verification.verifier_needed: Claim<->figure support check: read curve maxima off Figure 3 (argmax of both traces).
verification.rationale: Figure 3 read at 300 DPI: red relative-PLQY rises to a sharp maximum (~4.2) at 1.0% then falls; blue lifetime-histogram-max (plotted x2) also peaks (~4.0) at 1.0%. Both maxima coincide at 1.0%, directly supporting the prose. Evidence binds cleanly.

claims.text: The Shockley-Queisser limit caps single-junction PCE at 33.7%.
claims.quote: the power conversion efficiency (PCE) of single-junction cells is capped at 33.7% by the Shockley-Queisser (SQ) limit [1]
claims.predicate: Compare the stated 33.7% against the established detailed-balance SQ limit value.

evidence.quote: capped at 33.7% by the Shockley-Queisser (SQ) limit

verification.verifier_needed: Fixed-external-knowledge lookup of the SQ detailed-balance limit (~33.7% at ~1.34 eV, AM1.5).
verification.rationale: 33.7% is the widely cited single-junction SQ maximum (AM1.5G, optimal bandgap ~1.34 eV); matches established value. T1 knowledge check; no figure binding needed.

claims.text: Considering Auger recombination, the realistic single-junction efficiency limit for silicon is 29.4%.
claims.quote: considering Auger recombination [2], realistically limited to 29.4% for silicon (Si)
claims.predicate: Compare 29.4% against the Richter/Hermle/Glunz 2013 reassessed crystalline-silicon limit (ref [2]).

evidence.quote: realistically limited to 29.4% for silicon (Si)

verification.verifier_needed: Fixed-external-knowledge / literature lookup of ref [2] (Richter et al., IEEE J. Photovolt. 2013) Si efficiency limit.
verification.rationale: 29.4% is the canonical Richter et al. (2013) Auger-limited c-Si efficiency, the standard value cited and matching ref [2]. T1 knowledge check.

claims.text: Silicon has a bandgap of 1.12 eV, suitable as a tandem bottom cell.
claims.quote: Silicon, with its suitable bandgap of 1.12 eV, serves as a good bottom cell
claims.predicate: Compare stated 1.12 eV against the known room-temperature indirect bandgap of crystalline silicon.

evidence.quote: its suitable bandgap of 1.12 eV

verification.verifier_needed: Fixed-external-knowledge lookup of the c-Si bandgap (~1.12 eV at 300 K).
verification.rationale: 1.12 eV is the textbook room-temperature indirect bandgap of crystalline silicon. T1 knowledge check.

claims.text: The current certified world-record perovskite/silicon tandem efficiency obtained by LONGi stands at 35.0% PCE.
claims.quote: The current certified world record obtained by LONGi stands at 35.0% PCE (see NREL-chart).
claims.predicate: Cross-check the 35.0% LONGi perovskite/Si tandem record against the NREL certified efficiency chart / press records as of the paper's date.

evidence.quote: certified world record obtained by LONGi stands at 35.0% PCE

verification.verifier_needed: External literature / NREL-chart lookup of the certified perovskite-Si tandem record attributed to LONGi.
verification.rationale: Plausible and consistent with reported tandem progress (LONGi reported ~33-34% in 2023-2024; ~35% is within the trajectory by early 2026). Requires NREL-chart verification with a date anchor; not settleable from the paper alone. Internally consistent (used again as '35.0%' later).

claims.text: Mariotti et al. achieved a certified 32.5% efficiency in triple-halide perovskite-silicon tandem solar cells in 2022.
claims.quote: Mariotti et al. [6] achieved a certified efficiency of 32.5% in triple-halide perovskite-silicon tandem solar cells in 2022.
claims.predicate: Cross-check 32.5% certified tandem efficiency and the 2022 date against ref [6] (Mariotti et al., Science 2023).

evidence.quote: certified efficiency of 32.5% ... in 2022

verification.verifier_needed: Literature lookup of ref [6] (Mariotti et al., Science 381, 63-69, 2023) for the certified 32.5% value and date.
verification.rationale: 32.5% certified HZB/Mariotti triple-halide tandem matches the well-known result; however the paper dates it '2022' while ref [6] is published 2023 (work/announcement was 2022, publication 2023) - a minor date-attribution ambiguity worth flagging but not necessarily an error. Value itself is literature-checkable.

evidence.quote: addition of 2% (Fo-) formate anions significantly increases cell efficiency to 25.2%

claims.text: The theoretical efficiency limit for perovskite/silicon tandem cells is ~44% (also stated as ~45% and with a practical target of ~41%).
claims.quote: there remains significant room for improvement towards the theoretical limit of 44%. The gap between this value and the current "state of the art" (35.0% PCE) implies plenty of options for contributions on the road to a practical value [3] of ~41% PCE.
claims.predicate: Check internal consistency of the quoted theoretical-limit figures: abstract/intro use ~45% (p1), then 44% and ~41% (p2). Determine whether these are mutually consistent or conflicting framings.

theoretical limit of 44% ... practical value [3] of ~41% PCE

verification.rationale: Two different theoretical-limit numbers (~45% on p1 and 44% on p2) are given for tandem PCE, both ultimately leaning on ref [3] (Futscher & Ehrler 2016). This is a mild internal inconsistency / loose rounding rather than a hard arithmetic error; flag as suspect pending the ref [3] value (~44-45% is the commonly cited detailed-balance perovskite/Si tandem limit). Worth an internal-consistency note.

claims.quote: the current world record (27.2% PCE) [31] contains potassium binoxalate (K+ [HC2 O4 ]- , 0.7 mol%, 1.5 mg/mL) as well as formamidinium formate ([CH(NH2 )]+ [HCOO]- ), 0.65 mg/mL; 0.007 M; 0.4 mol% vs. 1.8 M Pb) as additives.

The 27.2% single-junction record and other additive specifics are T5 literature claims (ref [31]); plausible and consistent with late-2025 records, but exact attribution needs the cited paper.

claims.text: The XRD pattern of the Zn-Fo-doped film (1% doping) closely resembles Mariotti's digitized GIWAXS-derived pattern, indicating doping does not change the perovskite lattice structure.
claims.quote: The XRD pattern obtained is compared with the data reported by Mariotti and shows good resemblance ... This indicates that the incorporation of Zn and formate (at 1% total doping level) does not change the perovskite lattice structure
claims.predicate: Inspect Figure 2: compare peak positions (2-theta) of the red doped-film pattern against the black digitized Mariotti pattern; check qualitative correspondence and that no new dopant phases appear in the bulk pattern.

evidence.quote: XRD pattern of Zn-Fo-doped thin film ... (in red) on quartz ... together with the (optimized and digitized) XRD pattern ... as reported by Mariotti et al. [6] (black)

verification.verifier_needed: Claim<->figure support: visual/peak-position comparison of the two XRD patterns (and SI Table S1 peak list) to confirm lattice-invariance and absence of impurity phases.
verification.rationale: Figure 2 shows the doped (red) and digitized-Mariotti (black) patterns overlaid with apparent main-peak correspondence and an 'undoped' label; at rendered resolution the major perovskite peaks broadly align, but rigorous peak-position matching requires SI Table S1 (tabulated XRD). The black pattern is a bulk/GIWAXS-derived trace lacking the PbI2 surface peak (the authors note this), so the comparison is partly apples-to-oranges. Binding to a quantitative verdict needs the SI table.

claims.text: PbI2 needles were observed in the top layer of non-optimized films by both SEM and optical microscopy, and were eliminated by compositional engineering in optimized films.
claims.quote: PbI2 needles were observed in the top layer of our (non-optimized) films ... the optimized perovskite films do not exhibit visible PbI2 -related features at the surface
claims.predicate: Inspect Figure 4 (SEM vs optical) and Figure 6 inset: confirm white needle-like features in the non-optimized images and their absence in the optimized image, supporting the elimination claim.

evidence.location: Figure 4 (SEM + optical, scale bar 5 um / 20 um)
evidence.quote: Comparison of SEM and optical microscopy images ... containing substantial PbI2 needles in the top part of the layer (white crystals) ... No PbI2 is visible.
The inset shows the absence of PbI2 needles using optical microscopy for a wide-bandgap material (red time trace).

verification.verifier_needed: Claim<->figure (image) support: qualitative inspection that needle features present in non-optimized and absent in optimized images (corroborated by SI Figures S2-S5).
verification.rationale: Figure 4 right panel clearly shows white needle/stripe features (SEM stripes co-located with optical stripes) labelled as PbI2, and the left/inset panels show featureless perovskite without such needles. The qualitative morphological claim is image-supported; full verification (and that the needles are PbI2, not artifacts) would use SI images and XRD/EDX, which are not in the main text.

claims.text: Laminated 1%-doped optimized films showed no yellowing after more than 1 year under dark/indoor-light ambient conditions, but laser irradiation (532 nm, 1-5 suns, partial illumination) caused permanent photodegradation after 500 h.
claims.quote: no yellowing after more than 1 year under dark, or indoor light, ambient conditions ... laser irradiation with 532 nm through a pinhole or a slit ... showed clear signs of permanent photodegradation after 500 hrs. Laser power was varied from 1 to 5 suns.

claims.quote: Perovskite ... precursor solutions (1.4 M) were prepared by dissolving FAI, MACl, CsI, PbI2 , PbBr2 , and PbCl2 in 600 uL DMF and 150 uL DMSO according to the stoichiometric ratios

claims.text: Films were spin-coated at 5000 rpm for 45 s (5 s acceleration) with 300 uL anisole anti-solvent dropped at 25 s, then annealed at 100 C for 20 min.

verification.rationale: Drop time 25 s < 45 s total spin (consistent); 5000 rpm/45 s, anisole antisolvent, 100 C/20 min anneal are all within standard ranges for this composition. No internal inconsistency; appropriateness judgement, not a hard recompute.

claims.text: PLQY was measured at 450 nm excitation (Edinburgh FS5 + SC-30 integrating sphere), while long-lifetime TR-PL used 485 nm excitation (AMOLF) and composition-series TRPL used 488 nm (PicoQuant confocal).

verification.rationale: Three different instruments use 450/485/488 nm respectively; all are above-bandgap for a wide-bandgap (~1.7 eV) perovskite and appropriate. Figure 6 (485 nm) matches the AMOLF method; no contradiction. Minor: multiple wavelengths could confuse a reader but are consistently attributed per instrument.

claims.text: PbI2 is a non-photoactive material that does not produce photovoltage or photocurrent, and is preferentially enriched near the surface of the perovskite layer (per Mariotti GIWAXS).

verification.rationale: Domain-accepted that residual PbI2 is wide-gap and largely non-contributing to photocurrent in these devices (slight passivation effects aside); the 'preferentially enriched near surface' detail is attributed to ref [6] and is consistent with the literature. Established-knowledge support; the absolute 'does not produce any photocurrent' is a mild over-statement (PbI2 has ~2.3 eV gap, weak response) but qualitatively correct.

claims.text: Figure 3 right y-axis plots the maximum of the PL-lifetime histogram multiplied by 2 (x2 scaling).
claims.quote: maximum of PL-lifetime (us) histogram (x2)
claims.predicate: Check that the Figure 3 blue-axis '(x2)' scaling is internally consistent: the displayed peak (~4.0) implies an actual histogram-max of ~2.0 us at 1% doping, which should be reconcilable with the confocal (not AMOLF tail-fit) measurement basis.

evidence.quote: maximum of PL-lifetime (us) histogram (x2)

verification.verifier_needed: Internal cross-consistency: reconcile Figure 3 confocal histogram-max (~2 us after dividing by 2) with the AMOLF stretched-exp value (6.84 us) - distinct methods/quantities.
verification.rationale: The '(x2)' label means the plotted blue values are doubled; peak ~4.0 -> ~2 us actual histogram-max at 1% doping. This is a different metric (confocal lifetime-histogram peak, ref Figure S1) from the AMOLF stretched-exponential 6.84 us, so the two need not match. Without the SI histogram (Figure S1) the exact reconciliation can't be bound; flag as a potential reader-confusion point, not a clear error.

claims.text: Films were produced on ultra-flat glass coated with a 20 nm synthetic quartz layer, with representative film ~500 nm thick on a 15 x 20 mm substrate.
claims.quote: Ultra-flat glass substrates coated with a 20 nm synthetic quartz layer were obtained from Ossila ... Photograph of a representative wide-bandgap perovskite (~500 nm thick) layer on quartz (15 x 20 mm).
claims.predicate: Assess internal consistency/plausibility of substrate (20 nm quartz on glass) and film thickness (~500 nm) descriptions; check the 15 x 20 mm dimension against the Figure 1 photograph.

evidence.quote: (~500 nm thick) layer on quartz (15 x 20 mm); 20 nm synthetic quartz layer

verification.verifier_needed: Method-plausibility / internal-consistency check (film thickness ~500 nm typical; 'quartz' here is a 20 nm coating on glass, relevant when comparing to bulk-quartz literature).
verification.rationale: ~500 nm perovskite is standard; the substrate is glass with a 20 nm quartz coating (not bulk quartz) - worth noting since PLQY/lifetime are repeatedly described as 'on quartz'. Dimensions plausible vs Figure 1. Appropriateness judgement; no contradiction found.

claims.text: The green (2019) reference trace in Figure 6 corresponds to FA0.85MA0.15Pb(I0.95Br0.05)3 with 2.5% Zn(II) doping (caption text adds '2.5% Zn + CPACl').
claims.quote: green = FA0.85 MA0.15 Pb(I0.95 Br0.05 )3 with 2.5% Zn(II) doping, maximum at 800 nm.
claims.predicate: Check consistency between the Figure 6 caption ('2.5% Zn(II) doping') and the in-figure legend label, which reads '[2.5% Zn + CPACl]' - do the additive descriptions agree?

evidence.quote: green = FA0.85 MA0.15 Pb(I0.95 Br0.05 )3 with 2.5% Zn(II) doping

verification.verifier_needed: Prose(caption)-vs-figure(legend) consistency check of the green-trace additive description.
verification.rationale: The in-figure legend labels the green (2019) sample '[2.5% Zn + CPACl]' whereas the caption describes it as '2.5% Zn(II) doping' (no mention of CPACl), and the body (p8) calls it simply 'Zn-doped'. The Zn percentage (2.5%) is consistent, but the CPACl additive appears in the figure legend yet is dropped from the caption/body - a mild prose-vs-figure mismatch worth flagging.

paper_level_notes: Published MDPI Molecules 2026, 31, 625 (DOI 10.3390/molecules31040625); Wang, al Moubayed, Williams (Univ. Amsterdam). Open-access CC BY. Received 14 Jan 2026, published 11 Feb 2026; PDF created 16 Feb 2026 (note: PDF creation post-dates the stated publication date by 5 days - minor metadata oddity). Data availability: main results in article + Supplementary Materials (SI not bundled in this 12-page PDF); additional RDM data deposited at https://doi.org/10.21942/uva.31231672 (good reproducibility signal). Funding CSC project 202406140035; no conflicts declared. Many quantitative claims (XRD Table S1; PL lifetime histograms Fig S1; SEM/optical Fig S2-S5; UV-Vis+PL Fig S6; violin plots Fig S7; photodegradation Fig S8-S9) live in the SI and CANNOT be bound to evidence from this PDF alone - the strongest external-verification gains require fetching the SI and the RDM dataset. DETERMINISTIC FRONTIER (T0-T2) is the most checkable: the composition re-write (c3) recomputes essentially exactly (Cs 0.2095/FA 0.7429/MA 0.0476; I 0.8095/Br 0.1429/Cl 0.0476 -> stated 0.21/0.74/0.05 and 0.81/0.14/0.05), subscript sums = 1.00 (c1,c2), doping 0.5+0.5=1% (c4), DMF:DMSO 4:1 (c26), and the embedded 0.007 M / 1.8 M = 0.39 mol% ~ 0.4 mol% (c17) all hold. Cross-consistency mostly clean: PLQY 14% and lifetimes 6.84/3.65/1.57 us match across body, figures, and captions (c5,c7); abstract ~7 us = 6.84 us rounding (c31). MINOR FLAGS (suspect/worth-noting, NOT manufactured errors): (i) theoretical tandem limit quoted as both ~45% (p1) and 44% (p2), both leaning on ref [3] (c15); (ii) Figure 6 green-trace additive labelled '2.5% Zn + CPACl' in-plot but '2.5% Zn(II)' only in the caption/body (c34); (iii) Mariotti certified result dated '2022' in text but ref [6] is Science 2023 (c13); (iv) Figure 3 right axis is plotted x2 and reflects a confocal histogram-max (~2 us) distinct from the AMOLF 6.84 us, a possible reader-confusion point (c32); (v) 'on quartz' actually means a 20 nm quartz coating on glass (c33); (vi) ref [30] cited as Nat. Photon. 2025 vol 20 (forward-volume style) - citation-metadata check. No integrity red flags (T7) detected from the main text; numbers are self-consistent and reuse cleanly. Several T5 literature-record claims (35.0% LONGi, 32.5% Mariotti, 25.2% Jeong, 27.2%/26.95% records, 34.58% SAM tandem) are plausible and consistent with the known field trajectory but require literature/NREL-chart verification with a date anchor to settle.
